failed_assertions.check: Output includes an explicit style direction (e.g., 'minimalist', 'bold', 'modern', 'elegant', 'premium')
Output includes at least one specific hex color code or named color palette
Output specifies a target audience (e.g., 'tech executives', 'CTOs', 'engineering leaders')
Output does NOT contain vague phrases like 'make it nice', 'create a good design', or 'make it look professional' without further specifics
Output mentions whitespace, spacing, clean layout, or breathing room in the design
Output recommends or uses request-outline-review before generate-design-structured, or explicitly mentions reviewing the outline first
Output specifies the audience role and knowledge level (e.g., 'board members', 'C-suite', 'executives familiar with SaaS metrics')
Output follows the one-idea-per-slide principle or explicitly states each slide should have a single key message
Output specifies or recommends a length category ('short', 'balanced', 'long') or mentions a specific slide count range
The topic description is specific (references Q4, revenue, SaaS, board) rather than generic like 'quarterly update'
Output suggests generating multiple candidates or variations (e.g., 'generate 4 candidates', 'create several options')
Output mentions scalability, works at different sizes, favicon compatibility, or readability at small sizes
Output includes specific color guidance with hex codes or a described palette mood (not just 'use nice colors')
Output includes a clear style direction appropriate for fintech (e.g., 'modern', 'trustworthy', 'clean', 'professional')
Output correctly identifies the design_type as 'logo' or uses the appropriate Canva tool for logo generation
Output specifies text hierarchy with different sizes or emphasis levels (headline, subtitle, details)
Output describes specific layout intent (e.g., 'hero image at top', 'centered headline', 'details at bottom') rather than just 'a poster'
Output includes the event name ('CodeJam 2026') and dates (March 15-16) in the design plan
Output does NOT include or suggest placeholder text like 'Lorem ipsum', 'Your text here', or 'Event Name Here'
Output specifies physical dimensions, poster format, or intended display context (print size, digital display)
Output specifies Instagram-specific dimensions (1080x1080, 1080x1350) or mentions platform constraints
Output ensures visual cohesion across slides (consistent colors, fonts, or template across all 5 slides)
Output limits the color palette to 3 primary colors plus neutrals, or describes a cohesive limited palette
Output describes specific content for each slide or tip rather than treating all 5 as identical
Output mentions text readability, contrast, font size for mobile viewing, or avoiding text overlapping images

test_cases.prompt: Create a LinkedIn post announcement for a new AI product launch targeting tech executives

assertion_results.check: Output includes an explicit style direction (e.g., 'minimalist', 'bold', 'modern', 'elegant', 'premium')
Output includes at least one specific hex color code or named color palette
Output specifies a target audience (e.g., 'tech executives', 'CTOs', 'engineering leaders')
Output does NOT contain vague phrases like 'make it nice', 'create a good design', or 'make it look professional' without further specifics
Output mentions whitespace, spacing, clean layout, or breathing room in the design

test_cases.prompt: Generate a 10-slide presentation deck for a Q4 board meeting about SaaS revenue growth

assertion_results.check: Output recommends or uses request-outline-review before generate-design-structured, or explicitly mentions reviewing the outline first
Output specifies the audience role and knowledge level (e.g., 'board members', 'C-suite', 'executives familiar with SaaS metrics')
Output follows the one-idea-per-slide principle or explicitly states each slide should have a single key message
Output specifies or recommends a length category ('short', 'balanced', 'long') or mentions a specific slide count range
The topic description is specific (references Q4, revenue, SaaS, board) rather than generic like 'quarterly update'

test_cases.prompt: Design a logo for a fintech startup called 'NovaPay' that handles digital payments

assertion_results.check: Output suggests generating multiple candidates or variations (e.g., 'generate 4 candidates', 'create several options')
Output mentions scalability, works at different sizes, favicon compatibility, or readability at small sizes
Output includes specific color guidance with hex codes or a described palette mood (not just 'use nice colors')
Output includes a clear style direction appropriate for fintech (e.g., 'modern', 'trustworthy', 'clean', 'professional')
Output correctly identifies the design_type as 'logo' or uses the appropriate Canva tool for logo generation

test_cases.prompt: Create a poster for a university hackathon event called 'CodeJam 2026' happening March 15-16

assertion_results.check: Output specifies text hierarchy with different sizes or emphasis levels (headline, subtitle, details)
Output describes specific layout intent (e.g., 'hero image at top', 'centered headline', 'details at bottom') rather than just 'a poster'
Output includes the event name ('CodeJam 2026') and dates (March 15-16) in the design plan
Output does NOT include or suggest placeholder text like 'Lorem ipsum', 'Your text here', or 'Event Name Here'
Output specifies physical dimensions, poster format, or intended display context (print size, digital display)

test_cases.prompt: Create an Instagram carousel (5 slides) about '5 Tips for Remote Work Productivity' for a lifestyle brand

assertion_results.check: Output specifies Instagram-specific dimensions (1080x1080, 1080x1350) or mentions platform constraints
Output ensures visual cohesion across slides (consistent colors, fonts, or template across all 5 slides)
Output limits the color palette to 3 primary colors plus neutrals, or describes a cohesive limited palette
Output describes specific content for each slide or tip rather than treating all 5 as identical
Output mentions text readability, contrast, font size for mobile viewing, or avoiding text overlapping images
